questions.q: Vladimir Nabokov's 'Pale Fire' employs which narrative structure that consists of a poem followed by an editor's commentary and index?

David Foster Wallace's sprawling novel 'Infinite Jest' is set in a near-future North America where years are sponsored by corporations. What is the year called in the novel?
choices: Year of the Depend Adult Undergarment

questions.q: Which author, famous for novels set in the Raj, wrote 'The Jewel in the Crown' as the first book of 'The Raj Quartet'?

Primo Levi's 'If This Is a Man' is a Holocaust memoir that concludes with which famous question about human nature?
choices: What is the measure of a man?

questions.q: Thomas Pynchon's debut novel 'V.' employs which narrative technique where multiple storylines converge on a mysterious entity?

Georges Perec's 'A Void' is a lipogram—a novel written entirely without a certain letter. Which letter is absent throughout the entire text?

choices: The newspaper account of an enslaved woman named Margaret Garner

questions.q: Which Japanese author's 1994 novel 'Norwegian Wood' became one of the best-selling novels of all time despite initial critical dismissal?
David Foster Wallace's sprawling 1996 novel 'Infinite Jest' is set primarily in a near-future North America and features which fictional entertainment medium as a plot device?
choices: A film so entertaining it causes psychological harm

questions.q: In Jorge Luis Borges' famous short story 'The Aleph,' the protagonist encounters a point in space that contains which impossible quality?
choices: All points in the universe simultaneously
The thoughts of all living people

questions.q: Kazuo Ishiguro won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2017 primarily for works that explore which thematic concern across his novels?
choices: Memory, identity, and the unreliable nature of human recollection
